answer: Microsoft's annual revenue for fiscal 2024 was $245.1 billion, a 16% increase from the previous year, driven by strong cloud services performance. The company's net income rose to $88.1 billion, marking a 22% annual increase.

results.title: Microsoft Reports $64.7 Billion Revenue in Q4 FY24, 15% Increase
results.snippet: # Microsoft Reports $64.7 Billion Revenue in Q4 FY24, Marking 15% Increase. Microsoft had total revenue of $64.7 billion in the fourth quarter of the financial year 2024 (Q4FY24) ending on June 30. A large portion of the revenue came from Microsoft Cloud, whose revenue stood at $36.8 billion, up 21% year-on-year. **Microsoft’s net income:** The company’s net income for the quarter was $22 billion, which is a 10% year-on-year increase. **Revenue from Intelligent Cloud:** This segment consists of products such as Azure, SQL Server, Windows Server, and GitHub. It earned $28.5 billion this quarter, marking a 19% year-on-year increase. It earned a revenue of $20.3 billion an 11% year-on-year increase. Within the segment, Office commercial products and cloud services revenue increased by 12%. Office consumer products and cloud service revenue increased by 3% this quarter as Microsoft 365 subscribers reached 82.5 million. This segment had a revenue of $15.9 billion this quarter, which is 14% higher than the same quarter last year.

results.title: Microsoft revenue hits $245 billion | The Business Standard
results.snippet: Microsoft revenue hits $245 billion. ## By The Numbers. ## Microsoft reports $245 billion revenue for Fiscal Year ended June 30, 2024, a 16% increase, driven by strong performance in cloud services and other key sectors. Microsoft revenue reached $245 billion in 2024, showing strong growth. Image of Microsoft headquarters with revenue increase graphic. Microsoft's revenue hits $245B in 2024! Microsoft's revenue climbed to $245 billion in 2024, up from $212 billion the previous year, which represents a 16% increase. As the world's second-largest company by market cap, the company reported net income of $88.1 billion in 2024, posting an impressive 22% annual increase. Microsoft's 2024 revenue breakdown showing Azure as the top earner, followed by Office, Windows, Gaming (Xbox), and LinkedIn. Revenue percentages shown. Azure leads Microsoft's revenue in 2024 | Illustration: Ashrafun naher Ananna. #### Top News. By the numbers / Microsoft / revenue. Published comments are readers’ own views and The Business Standard does not endorse any of the readers’ comments.

results.title: Microsoft's Financial Results in FY24 Q4 – AGOLUTION
results.snippet: # FY24 Q4 Microsoft generates almost $65 Billion in Revenue And is one of the leading Companies in the Era of AI. * Microsoft’s **revenue** amounted to **$64.7 billion -** and increased by **15%.**. ## Solid Fiscal Year with Record Bookings and a strong Increase in Cloud Revenue. “We closed out our fiscal year with a solid quarter, highlighted by **record bookings** and **Microsoft Cloud quarterly revenue of $36.8 billion,** up 21% (up 22% in constant currency) year-over-year,” added Amy Hood, Executive Vice President and CFO of Microsoft. **Dynamics products and cloud services revenue** was **16% higher** in FY24 Q4 compared to the same quarter of the previous year, driven by **Dynamics 365 revenue** growth of **19%.**. In the **Intelligent Cloud** segment, **revenue amounted to $28.5 billion,** which was an **increase of 19%.**. * Microsoft achieved **total revenue of $245.1 billion,** an **increase of 16%.**.

results.title: Microsoft reports $56.5 billion in revenue for FY24 Q1, declines in ...
results.snippet: Microsoft reports $56.5 billion in revenue for FY24 Q1, declines in Surface offset by increases in cloud, Office, and Xbox.
